mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 7 Series 745e engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 745e engine (B58B30M2 with hybrid system) typically costs £3,000‑£4,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,200‑£1,800. As a PHEV, the hybrid system components add complexity and cost. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 7 Series 745e engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58 is a robust and refined engine – part of BMW's B‑family, known for its reliability. Common issues include coolant leaks (expansion tank, water pump) and PCV valve failure. The plug‑in hybrid system adds complexity – HV battery degradation and charging system failures are the primary PHEV‑specific concerns. With regular maintenance and proper battery care, the 745e can be a reliable and refined powertrain.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 7 Series 745e?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common issues include B58 coolant leaks (expansion tank, water pump, plastic fittings), PCV valve failure causing rough running and smoke, HV battery degradation (reduced electric range), and KLE module/charging system failure. The B58 is fundamentally robust – these are known but manageable issues.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a 745e engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Given the 7 Series's retained value (typically £20,000‑£45,000+), a £3,000‑£5,500 reconditioned engine is a modest fraction of the vehicle's worth. Replacement is almost always financially viable. However, if the HV battery has also failed (costing £2,000‑£5,000+), the total repair cost may be substantial. Get a full diagnostic assessment before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my 7 Series's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 745e uses the B58B30M2 3.0‑litre turbo petrol engine combined with an 83 kW electric motor. This is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C. The 745e badge itself confirms the powertrain – it's the 394 PS plug‑in hybrid offered in the G11/G12 7 Series LCI from 2019‑2022.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair a 745e engine issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues – B58 coolant leaks, PCV valve failure, charging system failures – are repairable. HV battery degradation may be covered under warranty if the vehicle is still within the warranty period (typically 8 years/100,000 miles). However, if the engine suffers major internal failure, a full engine replacement is usually more cost‑effective than a rebuild.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the G11/G12 745e generation. The hybrid system components (electric motor, battery, inverter) add complexity and cost to any replacement. PHEV‑specific labour is typically higher than for conventional engines.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 745e engine typically takes 14‑18 hours at £75‑£110/hr (independent BMW specialist). Hybrid system integration adds to the complexity.

cta.label: → Compare 7 Series 745e engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: B58 Coolant Leaks — Expansion Tank & Water Pump
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss without visible external leaks, engine overheating, warning lights, low coolant level message.
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,500 [PLACEHOLDER] (expansion tank, water pump, or coolant lines replacement).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored coolant loss can cause overheating and engine damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant loss or overheating occurs.

problems.recommendation: The B58 is a robust engine, but coolant leaks from the expansion tank, water pump, and plastic fittings are a common issue. The B58's water pump is driven by the serpentine belt and can fail. If the expansion tank or water pump develops a leak, the engine can overheat rapidly. A £500–£1,500 cooling system repair is far cheaper than the consequences of a warped head or blown head gasket. Regular inspection of coolant levels is essential.

problems.title: B58 PCV Valve Failure
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, engine misfire, blue/white smoke from exhaust, engine warning light, whistling noise from the engine.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored PCV failure can lead to oil consumption and catalytic converter damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms develop.

problems.recommendation: The B58's PCV valve is a known weak point. A failing PCV valve can cause rough running, engine misfires, and blue/white smoke from the exhaust due to oil being drawn into the intake. If the PCV valve fails, the engine may run rough and consume oil. Early diagnosis and replacement are essential — ignoring it can lead to more expensive repairs.

problems.title: HV (High‑Voltage) Battery Degradation (PHEV)
problems.symptoms: Reduced electric‑only range (noted drop from 35 miles to 20‑25 miles over time), longer charging times, hybrid system warnings on the dashboard, the car running only on petrol.
problems.typicalMileage: 30,000‑60,000 miles (age‑related as much as mileage — the 12 kWh battery is subject to degradation over time).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — battery replacement only (if ignored, the vehicle runs on petrol only, reducing fuel economy).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if HV battery warnings appear or if electric range drops significantly.

problems.recommendation: The 745e's 12 kWh lithium‑ion battery is a significant component. Over time, battery degradation is inevitable — the range will drop, and charging may take longer. BMW batteries are generally well‑engineered, but if the battery fails outside of the warranty period (typically 6‑8 years), replacement is expensive. If you notice a significant drop in electric range or warning lights, have the battery health checked by a specialist.

problems.title: Charging System / KLE Module Failure
problems.symptoms: The car fails to charge or charges very slowly, charging port faults, dashboard warnings, the car running only on petrol.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — can occur at any time.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if the car fails to charge.

problems.recommendation: The KLE (Comfort Charging Electronics Controller) module is a common failure point on BMW PHEVs. If the car fails to charge or charges slowly, the KLE module may be faulty. Charging port/socket faults are also reported. This is a specialist repair — a diagnostic check will confirm the issue. If your 745e won't charge, don't ignore it — the car will run on petrol only, negating the benefits of the hybrid system.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW 7 Series 745e — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Worth fixing — common and well‑understood repair
May be covered under warranty; if not, assess cost vs. vehicle value
Worth fixing — the car needs to charge to be a PHEV
Full engine replacement often more cost‑effective

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW 7 Series 745e? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with correct hybrid system maintenance
* HV battery health check completed and documented
* Evidence of regular charging and battery care
* Warranty remaining — the 745e is a complex, expensive vehicle to repair out of warranty
avoidIf: * Any sign of HV battery warnings or reduced electric range
* No evidence of regular charging — the hybrid battery may have been neglected
* Any sign of coolant loss — B58 cooling system issues are common
* Poor service history — these vehicles demand strict maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW 745e represents the refined entry point to BMW's electrified flagship range — combining the robust B58 3.0‑litre turbo petrol engine with an 83 kW electric motor for 394 PS and 600 Nm. It offers genuine electric‑only capability for short commutes (up to 35 miles) and effortless performance when the petrol engine is engaged. However, PHEV complexity brings potential issues — battery degradation, charging system failures, and the usual B58 cooling system concerns. Buy on service history above all else — a vehicle with documented battery health checks and regular maintenance is a safe bet; a neglected example can be expensive to repair.

cta.label: → Compare 7 Series 745e engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 7 Series 745e (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Electric Range — Does the displayed range match expectations? (35 miles claimed, real‑world ~20‑30 miles)
2. Service History — Full BMW history with hybrid system maintenance records
3. HV Battery Health — Has the battery been tested? What's its state of health?
4. Charging — Does the car charge correctly? (KLE module and charging port check)
5. Coolant Level — Any sign of coolant loss? (B58 cooling system common issue)
6. PCV System — Any rough idle, misfire, or smoke? (PCV valve common issue)
7. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any hybrid system or engine codes?
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures or any advisory notes

engineCodes.matchNote: This table's rows match Section 1B's Era Map exactly.
